generated_baseline.policy: Use generated reader source and reader overlays as the reconciliation baseline before any curated reader chapter is treated as release input.

overlay_source.policy: Prefer semantic overlays for section-local reader-only edits; graduate to curated manuscript files only when the edit is too broad for overlays.

graduation_criteria: Generated reader source plus overlays are reviewed and found insufficient for final human-reader prose.
A curated chapter file maps to exactly one manifest chapter ID.
Every curated chapter records its generated-reader baseline, live-source commit or tag, and reconciliation status.
Meaning-changing caveats, support-state limits, implementation horizons, and non-claim boundaries are preserved.
A reconciliation report exists before any reader artifact is rendered from curated source.
The release record names curated reader source only after review actually happens.

reader_handoff_contract.single_thesis: Advanced AI should be built as a governed stack of bounded capabilities, evidence states, executable contracts, resource-aware routes, and reviewable artifacts, so self-improvement can proceed only through visible claims, receipts, residuals, and rollback paths.

part_arcs.title: Part I - Foundations, Alignment, and Governance
part_arcs.reader_arc: The reader first learns why advanced intelligence needs boundaries, evidence discipline, contestable values, stable capability identity, security, and recursive-improvement limits before any impressive capability can be trusted.
part_arcs.reader_stakes: Without this part, the book would look like an engineering catalog rather than an answer to authority, failure, value conflict, and self-improvement risk.
part_arcs.handoff_payoff: By the end of the part, the reader should treat alignment as a governed control architecture rather than a promise attached to a model.

part_arcs.title: Part II - Planning, Memory, Reasoning, and Execution
part_arcs.reader_arc: The reader then follows an intent as it becomes executable work, planned steps, semantic representations, accountable context, verification records, belief updates, typed jobs, tool effects, and reusable procedures.
part_arcs.reader_stakes: Without this part, the stack would have principles but no accountable path from a human request to a completed artifact.
part_arcs.handoff_payoff: By the end of the part, the reader should see cognition as a chain of contracts whose failures remain inspectable instead of disappearing into chat output.

part_arcs.title: Part III - Routing, Compression, Representation, and Substrates
part_arcs.reader_arc: The third part explains how a governed stack chooses specialists, readiness states, compute locations, compression modes, speed paths, budgets, mathematical substrates, and cyclic memory structures without confusing efficiency with evidence.
part_arcs.reader_stakes: Without this part, the book would leave efficiency as a vague hope instead of a governed allocation problem with residuals and negative controls.
part_arcs.handoff_payoff: By the end of the part, the reader should understand that efficient ASI is a routing and representation discipline, not merely faster token production.

part_arcs.title: Part IV - Evidence, Implementation, and the Living Book
part_arcs.reader_arc: The final part turns the architecture back onto its own claims through proofs, benchmarks, governed feedback, stewardship, integration traces, implementation references, prototype sequence, publication method, and open research debt.
part_arcs.reader_stakes: Without this part, the book would ask for belief instead of showing how claims are tested, limited, corrected, and carried forward.
part_arcs.handoff_payoff: By the end of the part, the reader should know which pieces are argued, which are mechanized or measured, and what work remains before stronger claims are honest.

signature_ideas.label: Stack, Not Model
signature_ideas.reader_promise: Capability becomes safer and more useful when the reader can name the layer, boundary, receipt, and accountable owner of each action.

signature_ideas.label: Authority Before Action
signature_ideas.reader_promise: No component should act, delegate, expose data, or spend resources without an inspectable authority record and refusal route.

signature_ideas.label: Evidence States
signature_ideas.reader_promise: The book stays honest by separating what is imagined, argued, source-derived, tested, measured, mechanized, deprecated, or refuted.

signature_ideas.label: Residual Honesty
signature_ideas.reader_promise: Unresolved risk, verification debt, displaced cost, and uncertainty should remain visible rather than being hidden behind a successful output.

signature_ideas.label: Typed Work
signature_ideas.reader_promise: Intelligent work becomes governable when requests, jobs, effects, outputs, approvals, and audit events are typed artifacts rather than informal impressions.

signature_ideas.label: Accountable Memory
signature_ideas.reader_promise: Context is not just more tokens; it is state with provenance, scope, taint, deletion, adequacy, and downstream accountability.

signature_ideas.label: Routing as Governance
signature_ideas.reader_promise: Choosing a model, specialist, compute host, or method is itself an authority and evidence decision with costs and residuals.

signature_ideas.label: Transactional Improvement
signature_ideas.reader_promise: Self-improvement should look like controlled replacement, qualification, monitoring, rollback, and residual ownership rather than unchecked mutation.

signature_ideas.label: Proofs as Receipts
signature_ideas.reader_promise: Formal work should function as scoped receipts attached to claims, contracts, and replayable artifacts instead of decorative rigor.

signature_ideas.label: Living Book
signature_ideas.reader_promise: The book itself should behave like the system it describes: versioned, evidence-aware, reviewer-facing, and capable of honest correction.

corben_voice_pass_slots.prompt: Corben should supply the plain-language reason this architecture matters and the reader promise the opening must carry.
Corben should decide which real project pressures made authority boundaries and failure obligations feel non-negotiable.
Corben should provide the authorial language for why support states, demotions, and no-promotion decisions deserve reader trust.
Corben should choose the human stakes vocabulary for intent, dignity, corrigibility, moral uncertainty, and contestable governance.
Corben should state the desired reader feeling around upgrades, rollback, stable capability identity, and recursive improvement limits.
Corben should mark where concrete implementation memory can make contracts, jobs, adapters, artifact graphs, and procedures feel lived-in.
Corben should identify the most intuitive way to make accountable context, taint, verification bandwidth, and belief revision memorable.
Corben should supply the practical resource tradeoffs that make routing, owned compute, fast generation, compression, and budgets feel concrete.
Corben should decide how much wonder, restraint, and falsifiability belongs in the mathematical and cyclic substrate chapters.
Corben should choose the practical language for formal proofs as useful receipts rather than claims of total correctness.
Corben should provide project-history context for Theseus, reference traces, artifact stewardship, and prototype order where public-safe.
Corben should decide the closing invitation, unresolved questions, and reader handoff for the living-book method and open agenda.

non_claims: This handoff contract is not a reader release record.
This handoff contract does not promote any Appendix C support state.
This handoff contract does not fabricate Corben first-person experience, personal lessons, project history, conviction, or authorial approval.
This handoff contract does not create, approve, or review EPUB, PDF, DOCX, HTML, audio, or audio-embedded EPUB artifacts.

companion_note_routing.policy: Track chapter-level companion-note decisions for reader, e-reader, and audio review while preserving meaning-critical caveats in the reader spine.

chapter_review_matrix.policy: Track chapter-by-chapter human-reader review status, overlay disposition, companion-note candidates, curated-manuscript candidates, and release blockers while keeping manifest chapter identity synced to book_structure.json.

format_review_matrix.policy: Track reader format artifact review separately from chapter-text review, and keep every format blocked until artifact review and an edition release record actually exist.

curation_contract.policy: If generated reader source plus overlays stop being enough, curated chapter files may become a parallel derivative prose source only under this contract; they remain subordinate to live-book claims, support states, source boundaries, proof/test status, implementation horizons, and release records.

chapter_records.title: Artifact Graphs, Audit Logs, and Replay
chapter_records.reader_stakes: Readers need produced work to remain inspectable after completion, because outputs without provenance cannot carry durable evidence.
chapter_records.reader_payoff: The payoff is an artifact graph that links parent jobs, sources, context, tools, tests, audits, replay grades, and allowed reuse.
chapter_records.divergence_summary: First curated reader prose pass reframes the chapter around work that remains inspectable after production, artifact identity, parent jobs, source/context refs, context transactions, tool refs, audit events, replay grades, claim/test links, evidence gates, residuals, allowed reuse, minimum viable replay packets, a chapter-edit replay-packet scenario, and beyond-SOTA evidence graphs; no claim meaning, support state, source boundary, proof/test status, implementation horizon, or release approval is changed.

chapter_records.title: Human Intent as a Formal Input

chapter_records.title: Planning as a Control Layer: DAGs and Intelligence Arbitrage
chapter_records.reader_stakes: Readers must understand planning as control over work, evidence, budgets, and risk instead of a prettier chain-of-thought transcript.
chapter_records.reader_payoff: The payoff is a planning model where DAGs, arbitrage, dispatch, and stop conditions become inspectable governance surfaces.
chapter_records.divergence_summary: Curated reader prose now treats planning and PlanForge DAG/intelligence-arbitrage material as one chapter: planning as controlled delay before action, schedulable DAGs, node lifecycle states, blocked work, dispatch receipts, adequacy contracts, route assignments, cost-quality ledgers, residuals, Semantic IR handoff, schema/harness boundaries, and finite-record Planning plus PlanForge proof limits; no claim meaning, support state, source boundary, proof/test status, implementation horizon, or release approval is changed.

chapter_records.title: Proof-Carrying Claims and Adversarial Review
chapter_records.reader_stakes: Readers need verification and adversarial review to function as claim discipline without implying automatic truth or theorem-grade certainty.
chapter_records.reader_payoff: The payoff is a proof-carrying-claim model that keeps receipts, reviewers, counterarguments, and unresolved doubts attached to claims.

chapter_records.title: Compact Generative Systems: Generate, Verify, Repair, and Residual Honesty
chapter_records.reader_stakes: Readers need compact generation to preserve unresolved obligations instead of rewarding short outputs that conceal verification debt.
chapter_records.reader_payoff: The payoff is a generate-verify-repair frame where compression remains accountable to residuals, gates, and evidence boundaries.
chapter_records.divergence_summary: First curated reader pass strengthens the human-reader opening around residual honesty: small only counts after the remainder is named. The 2026-07-03 alignment updates the reader MVI to surface the implemented Compact GVR synthetic slice while preserving CGS, generate/verify/repair, semantic-leasing, proof/test, source, and support-state boundaries without adding implementation, codec, or benchmark claims.

chapter_records.title: RankFold, NeuralFold, and Artifact Compression
chapter_records.reader_stakes: Readers need artifact compression to remain deterministic, inspectable, and claim-bounded instead of becoming lossy authority laundering.
chapter_records.reader_payoff: The payoff is a compression frame where folded artifacts carry receipts, decoders, utility limits, and reconstruction boundaries.
chapter_records.divergence_summary: First curated reader pass strengthens the archive-facing opening around routed artifact compression: a compressed artifact earns one use at a time. It preserves RankFold/NeuralFold, BBVCA, CGS, source, proof/test, and support-state boundaries without adding compressor or benchmark claims.
